chapter_context.summary: David flees to the priests at Nob, obtaining holy showbread and Goliath's sword; he then flees to Gath and feigns madness to escape Achish.

outline.text: David Flees to Nob; Obtains Showbread and Goliath's Sword
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Flees to Nob; Obtains Showbread and Goliath's Sword (21:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: David Flees to Achish of Gath; Feigns Madness
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Flees to Achish of Gath; Feigns Madness (21:10-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

key_themes.title: Sovereignty of God
key_themes.description: God raises up the humble (Hannah, Samuel, David) and humbles the proud (Eli's sons, Saul).

key_themes.title: The Heart vs. Outward Appearance
key_themes.description: God looks at the inner spiritual character, not physical stature or social status.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 21, how does this encourage you to trust God when circumstances look bleak?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine the sincerity of your own heart before the LORD?

cultural_historical.title: Transition to Monarchy
cultural_historical.text: Israel's request for a king represents a major cultural shift from a tribal league led by temporary judges to a centralized dynastic monarchy.
